Palm Beach County Home Loans

FHA loans in Palm Beach County, Florida

Palm Beach County stretches from affluent coastal enclaves like Boca Raton to fast-growing, more affordable communities inland. As a designated high-cost county, its FHA limit sits above Florida's floor — which helps in a market where coastal prices run high. The trick is matching FHA to the right segment: condos and mid-market homes in places like West Palm Beach and Boynton Beach, where the program is genuinely workable.

Palm Beach County FHA limit (1-unit)Above floor

What the FHA limit means in Palm Beach County

Palm Beach County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is set above the $541,287 statewide floor — giving you more borrowing room than most of Florida. Because published figures vary for high-cost counties, we confirm Palm Beach County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup before you shop. Even with the higher limit, much of the county's luxury coastal inventory exceeds it — FHA buyers focus on condos and mid-market homes.

Coast vs. value markets in Palm Beach County

The eastern coast (Boca, Palm Beach, parts of West Palm) skews luxury and condo-heavy — for condos, confirm FHA approval or Single-Unit Approval via our condo guide. More attainable prices in Boynton Beach and the western communities let FHA's 3.5% down go further, especially for first-time buyers. Because the high-cost limit varies, we confirm the county's exact figure with HUD before you shop.

Cities We Serve

FHA loans in Palm Beach County cities

We help FHA buyers throughout Palm Beach County, including West Palm Beach, Boca Raton, Boynton Beach. Explore our local guides for a closer look at the market, loan limits, and down payment help in each:

West Palm BeachBoca RatonBoynton Beach

Down payment help in Palm Beach County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Palm Beach County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Palm Beach County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Palm Beach County for 2026?+
Palm Beach is a designated high-cost county, so its 2026 FHA limit is above the $541,287 Florida floor. Published figures vary, so confirm with HUD's official lookup.
Can I use FHA in expensive Boca Raton?+
Yes, in the right segments — condos and mid-market homes. Much of Boca's luxury market exceeds FHA limits, but the program works in parts of the city.
Where is FHA most workable in Palm Beach County?+
In value markets like Boynton Beach and the western communities, plus FHA-approved condos countywide.
How much down payment do I need in Palm Beach County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score; gift funds and seller credits help in a higher-priced market.
Do you serve all of Palm Beach County?+
Yes — West Palm Beach, Boca Raton, Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, Wellington, and every community in the county.
